summary: Completed testing and code review for Windows download/install process improvements. All requested features verified: DownloadGuideModal component with correct data-testid attributes, AccountDashboard integration, backend .exe preference, and CI/CD workflow updates.

notes: GET /api/download/windows works correctly - returns 307 redirect to GitHub releases
Backend prefers .exe over .zip (code verified at server.py lines 2639)
Currently redirects to .zip because GitHub release v1.0.8 only has .zip - .exe will be available after new CI/CD workflow runs
AccountDashboard requires Google OAuth authentication - modal cannot be tested via browser automation
All data-testid attributes present and correctly implemented

passed_tests: Backend: Download endpoint redirects to github.com/Behonkus/obsidian-cinema
Backend: Download endpoint returns valid installer extension (.exe or .zip)
Backend: Download redirect chain completes successfully
Backend: POST /api/download/windows returns 405 Method Not Allowed
Code Review: DownloadGuideModal.jsx - Pre-download state shows 'Download Installer (.exe)' button
Code Review: DownloadGuideModal.jsx - Post-download state shows 4 installation steps
Code Review: AccountDashboard.jsx - Modal rendered with onDownload prop (lines 269-277)
Code Review: server.py - Download endpoint prefers .exe over .zip (line 2639)
Code Review: build-windows.yml - No zip compression step
critical_code_review_comments: All required data-testid attributes implemented correctly
Modal has two states: pre-download (download button) and post-download (4 steps with numbered circles)
Step component dynamically generates data-testid='download-step-{number}' for steps 1-4
CI/CD workflow correctly uploads .exe directly without zip compression
context_for_next_testing_agent: DownloadGuideModal is behind Google OAuth authentication (AccountDashboard page). Backend /api/download/windows endpoint works correctly - currently redirects to .zip because GitHub release v1.0.8 only has .zip available. Once a new release is created via the updated build-windows.yml workflow, it will redirect to .exe. The workflow now uploads *.exe directly instead of creating a zip file.
notes_on_github_release: Current GitHub release (v1.0.8) only contains Obsidian-Cinema-Setup.zip. The updated CI/CD workflow will upload .exe directly in future releases. Backend code is ready to prefer .exe when available.
